name: Italian Neorealism
description: Post-war movement using non-actors and location shooting to depict working-class life.

name: French New Wave
description: Innovative movement rejecting classical Hollywood in favour of personal, reflexive filmmaking.

name: Japanese Golden Age
description: Period of extraordinary creative output in Japanese studio cinema led by Kurosawa and others.

name: Soviet Poetic Cinema
description: Soviet movement prioritising lyrical, non-narrative imagery over socialist realism.

name: New Hollywood
description: Director-driven American cinema challenging studio conventions with darker, ambiguous narratives.

name: Third Cinema
description: Political filmmaking from Latin America and Africa challenging colonialism and Western values.

name: Taiwanese New Wave
description: Contemplative, realist movement foregrounding Taiwanese social change and identity.

name: Hong Kong New Wave
description: Energetic Hong Kong movement blending genre cinema with personal artistic vision.

name: Iranian New Wave
description: Austere, humanist movement often focusing on children and the rural poor.

name: Structural Film
description: American avant-garde movement foregrounding film's material properties over narrative.

name: Feminist Cinema
description: Films by and for women, challenging the male gaze and conventional representations of femininity.

name: Bengali Parallel Cinema
description: Art-house Indian cinema outside the Bollywood mainstream, grounded in literary realism.
